clusters.headline: PostNL seeks looser postal delivery rules
clusters.summary: PostNL, the Dutch postal operator, has requested changes to the country's Universal Postal Service regulations, arguing that the legal obligations no longer reflect the shrinking postal market. The company reported a €35 million loss on the statutory service last year, citing the burden as unfair.

stories.title: PostNL wants laxer rules for statutory postal delivery task

clusters.headline: Suspect arrested after Wassenaar shooting
clusters.summary: Two people were injured when a shooting broke out at a home in Wassenaar early Sunday. Police arrested a suspect while investigating the incident. No further details on motive or weapon were provided.

stories.title: Video: Two injured in Wassenaar shooting; Suspect arrested

clusters.headline: Violence erupts at Ter Apel asylum centre
clusters.summary: Fights broke out at the entrance of the Ter Apel asylum application centre on the first evening after the Red Cross and VluchtelingenWerk withdrew assistance due to safety concerns. An individual was taken to hospital after becoming unwell.

stories.title: Video: Fights break out outside Ter Apel center on first night after aid groups pull out

clusters.headline: Persistent stink confirmed at Amsterdam prison
clusters.summary: Police confirmed that strong odours persist in the detention cells at the Elandsgracht police station in Amsterdam’s Centrum district. The facility, which houses around 45 cells, serves detainees from the Centrum, Noord and West boroughs, and cleaning crews have been unable to eliminate the smell.

stories.title: Police confirm persistent stink at Amsterdam prison

clusters.headline: Injection linked to higher brain tumour risk
clusters.summary: A study found that women who received the contraceptive injection for at least one year were 5.6 times more likely to develop meningioma brain tumours. The research highlights a potential health risk associated with long‑term use of the injection.

stories.title: ‘I have an inoperable brain tumour after having the contraceptive jab’

clusters.headline: Swans claim upset win over East Perth
clusters.summary: Swan Districts forward Leigh Kohlmann praised the club’s 19‑point victory over East Perth as its best win in two years. He said the result indicates significant progress for the team in Bassendean.

stories.title: Something’s brewing in Bassendean as Kohlmann hails Swans

clusters.headline: Footage shows death of World Cup star
clusters.summary: Footage of 25‑year‑old footballer Adams, who had played twice in the World Cup, surfaced after his death in Schotschekloof, Cape Town. The cause of death remains unconfirmed.

stories.title: Heartbreaking footage of tragic World Cup star emerges

clusters.headline: FIFA to consider 64‑team World Cup
clusters.summary: FIFA President Gianni Infantino said the governing body is exploring expanding the World Cup to 64 teams, arguing that every nation should have the opportunity to participate. The proposal would increase the tournament’s size beyond the current 32 teams.

stories.title: FIFA considers expanding World Cup to 64 teams, Infantino says

clusters.headline: Farage rating falls to lowest level
clusters.summary: An Opinium survey showed former UKIP leader Nigel Farage’s approval rating dropped to –27, the lowest since the general election. More than half of voters disapproved of him, while only a quarter approved.

stories.title: Farage's ratings plummet to lowest level since the general election

clusters.headline: Senator Lindsey Graham dies at 71
clusters.summary: US Senator Lindsey Graham died at 71 after a brief and sudden illness, according to his office. The senator had returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday.

stories.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness', his office says
Lindsey Graham dead at 71

clusters.headline: New COPD treatment offers hope to patients
clusters.summary: Samuel Maclean, a long‑term COPD patient, is among the first to benefit from a new treatment that claims to improve lung function and quality of life. The therapy is being promoted as a significant advance for sufferers.

stories.title: 'This new COPD treatment could make a real difference to patients like me'

clusters.headline: England advance past Norway in World Cup quarter-final
clusters.summary: England progressed to the World Cup semi-finals with a dramatic victory over Norway, though manager Thomas Tuchel criticised his team's performance. Jude Bellingham responded dismissively to Tuchel's comments, while celebrations in Piccadilly Circus led to multiple arrests. Bellingham also shared a lighthearted moment with Erling Haaland before the match.

stories.title: Thomas Tuchel's criticism of England stars and what Jude Bellingham said on ITV

Police arrest fans in Piccadilly Circus after England beat Norway
Jude Bellingham's 'cheeky' act towards Erling Haaland before England match

clusters.headline: Melbourne thrash Richmond in AFL clash
clusters.summary: Melbourne extended their perfect MCG record to 9-0 with a 46-point victory over Richmond in wet conditions, with Van Rooyen starring for the Demons. The win strengthened Melbourne's position in the top four of the AFL ladder.

stories.title: Van Rooyen stars as Dees dismantle struggling Tigers

clusters.headline: Elderly couple disappear during shopping trip
clusters.summary: Police are investigating the unexplained disappearance of an elderly couple who vanished during a routine shopping trip. Family members and authorities have expressed growing concern for the couple's welfare as searches continue.

stories.title: Couple vanish in shopping trip mystery

clusters.headline: Football transfer window updates dominate headlines
clusters.summary: Transfer activity continues across European football with Joao Gomes linked to Manchester United, Bradley Barcola's future under discussion, and Liverpool responding to market movements. Updates also cover Marcus Rashford and Bruno Guimaraes situations.

stories.title: Transfer news LIVE Joao Gomes to Man Utd, Barcola updates, Liverpool response

clusters.headline: Family dispute erupts over baby name spelling
clusters.summary: A woman sparked controversy after laughing at her cousin's baby daughter's unconventional name, leading to a heated family argument. The cousin responded sharply to the criticism, igniting debate about the chosen name's appearance.

stories.title: 'My cousin's baby name looks like a spelling mistake - it makes me laugh'

clusters.headline: Minneapolis restaurant adopts pay-what-you-can model
clusters.summary: Restaurant owner Dylan Alverson transformed Post Modern Times in Minneapolis to a pay-what-you-can pricing system after struggling to make traditional numbers work. The model has proven financially successful while serving the community.

stories.title: I couldn't make my restaurant's numbers work. So I took prices off the menu.

clusters.headline: Wimbledon curfew change and prize purse revealed
clusters.summary: Wimbledon's curfew rule, in place for 17 years, was changed for the singles final timing, causing issues; meanwhile the men's singles champion will receive a record prize purse as Jannik Sinner faces Alexander Zverev in the 2026 final.

stories.title: Wimbledon curfew explained as rule change causes issue for final
How much the Wimbledon men's singles champion earns for winning final

clusters.headline: Messi sends X-rated message to England players
clusters.summary: Lionel Messi's Argentina side will face England in the 2026 World Cup semi-finals after beating Switzerland; following the win, Messi posted an X-rated message directed at England players, sparking controversy ahead of the clash.

stories.title: Lionel Messi sends X-rated message to England players after Argentina win

clusters.headline: New footage emerges of deceased World Cup star
clusters.summary: Heartbreaking footage shows the tragic World Cup star Adams, 25, found dead in Cape Town weeks after playing in the tournament; cause of death remains unconfirmed, adding to existing coverage of the player's death.

stories.title: Heartbreaking footage of tragic World Cup star emerges

clusters.headline: High street offers cooling fashion for heatwave
clusters.summary: Shoppers can keep cool in the heat with breezy buys from Monsoon and other high street retailers, with items starting from as little as £18, as advice is given for dressing during the current heatwave.

stories.title: Dress for the heatwave with our top cooling fashion buys from Monsoon and more

clusters.headline: Eco-friendly liquid tackles stubborn weeds without vinegar
clusters.summary: Gardening expert Simon Akeroyd shares a simple, eco-friendly method to remove impossible-to-dig weeds from patios, driveways and gravel surfaces by pouring an effective liquid rather than using vinegar, offering a sustainable solution for gardeners.

stories.title: Kill 'impossible' weeds when pouring effective liquid – not vinegar

clusters.headline: Tourist shocked by Tenerife meal prices
clusters.summary: Ruben Chorlton-Owen, 25, from North Wales, visited Tenerife with his girlfriend and was taken aback by the high cost of food relative to prices back home, sharing his experience online to highlight the price disparity.

stories.title: Man orders meal in Tenerife but is floored by price of food

clusters.headline: Details released on Ann Widdecombe murder investigation
clusters.summary: Investigators say the 78-year-old former politician was found in a pool of her own blood inside the kitchen of her remote Dartmoor bungalow; police continue to examine the scene as they seek to establish the circumstances surrounding her death.

stories.title: Everything we know so far about Ann Widdecombe's murder

clusters.headline: Wid​decombe murder investigation updates
clusters.summary: Police have arrested a 26‑year‑old man in Rotherham on suspicion of murdering former MP Ann Widdecombe, ruling out a political motive. The 78‑year‑old former minister was found dead in her Devon bungalow, prompting reactions from the prime minister and opposition leader. Additional coverage includes a recap of her career, her final public appearance on TalkTV, and a detailed timeline of the probe.

clusters.summary: Two charged as body found in suitcase in woods — A man from London is charged with murder after a man's body is found in woods in Kent. — A man from London is charged with murder after a man's body is

clusters.headline: England reach World Cup semi-finals after Norway win
clusters.summary: England defeated Norway to reach the World Cup semi-finals against Argentina. Manager Thomas Tuchel criticised the performance, calling the win "lucky," but match-winner Jude Bellingham defended the team. Declan Rice played after three days ill with a virus. Fans celebrated in London while Prime Minister Keir Starmer hinted at a bank holiday if England win the tournament.

Tuchel angry at 'lucky' England - but Bellingham defends players

clusters.headline: Hillsborough Law bill set for MPs approval
clusters.summary: MPs are set to approve the Hillsborough Law bill, addressing the 1989 FA Cup semi-final crush that killed 97 Liverpool fans. The legislation aims to ensure public authorities cooperate with investigations and provide legal representation for bereaved families.

stories.title: Bill for Hillsborough Law set to be approved by MPs

clusters.headline: BBC reviews Brendon McCullum's Bazball era
clusters.summary: BBC Sport examines the highs and lows of Brendon McCullum's tenure as England Test head coach following his sacking. The aggressive "Bazball" approach brought initial success but ultimately led to his departure.

stories.title: The highs and lows of Bazball

clusters.headline: Exam board apologises for Sats results delay
clusters.summary: An exam board has apologised for delays to Sats results in England. A school leaders' union demands cast-iron assurances that final results will be accurate following the disruption.

stories.title: Exam board sorry for delay to Sats results in England

clusters.headline: Senator Lindsey Graham dies after brief illness
clusters.summary: Senator Lindsey Graham, 71, died after a brief and sudden illness, his office said. He had returned from Kyiv, meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday. Donald Trump paid tribute, calling Graham a true American patriot who will be greatly missed.

stories.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'

clusters.headline: BBC correspondent David Willey dies aged 93
clusters.summary: BBC foreign correspondent David Willey died aged 93. He reported on five popes and was best known for extensive Vatican coverage throughout his decades-long career, delivering authoritative international news globally.

stories.title: David Willey, esteemed BBC foreign correspondent, dies aged 93

clusters.headline: Former Qatar Emir dies aged 74
clusters.summary: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani died aged 74. He assumed power in 1996 after a bloodless coup and transformed Qatar into a gas‑rich nation shaping its modern development.

stories.title: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74

clusters.headline: Polish PM pledges WW2 genocide memorial
clusters.summary: Polish Prime Minister pledged a memorial to victims of World War Two, describing the killings as genocide by Ukrainian nationalists. Warsaw and Kyiv have long disputed the scale of civilian deaths during the war.

stories.title: Polish PM pledges memorial to victims of WW2 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists'

clusters.headline: Met police probe Reform election donations
clusters.summary: Metropolitan Police are investigating Reform UK over alleged election donation breaches involving the mother of convicted fraudster George Cottrell, according to media reports, raising concerns about party financing within the UK.

stories.title: Met police investigating Reform over election donations - reports

clusters.headline: France face Spain, England play Argentina in semi-finals
clusters.summary: The 2026 World Cup semi-finals will feature France against Spain and England against Argentina. The preview outlines key players, tactics, and expectations for the heavyweight matchups. Both matches are scheduled for July 2026 at the Los Angeles stadium, with the winner advancing to the final.

stories.title: What to expect in the semi-finals

clusters.headline: Jayden Adams death confirmed after footage emerges
clusters.summary: Footage of 25‑year‑old South African midfielder Jayden Adams surfaced after his death in Schotschekloof, Cape Town. Adams, who played for Mamelodi Sundowns and featured for South Africa at the World Cup weeks earlier, was found dead; the cause remains unconfirmed.

stories.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25

clusters.headline: World Cup and Wimbledon fashion quiz launched
clusters.summary: An interactive fashion test invites users to compare the style of the World Cup and Wimbledon, highlighting the high‑stakes attire associated with both events and encouraging participants to choose their favourite looks.

stories.title: Is the World Cup or Wimbledon more stylish? Take our fashion test to find out

clusters.headline: Jordan Bardella photo shows Le Pen party hierarchy
clusters.summary: A photograph reveals the internal ranking within Marine Le Pen's party, showing 30‑year‑old Jordan Bardella, previously groomed as the next hard‑right leader, now required to wait longer for a leadership role.

stories.title: The photo that shows who's really on top in Le Pen's party

clusters.headline: Elephant seal Neil sparks Australian mayhem
clusters.summary: Five‑year‑old elephant seal Neil attracted global attention during his recent haul ashore on southern Tasmania, where his presence caused disruptions and widespread media coverage across Australia.

stories.title: How a viral seal called Neil caused mayhem in Australia

clusters.headline: Australian telecom outage disrupts trains, emergency calls
clusters.summary: A major outage at Australia's largest telecommunications provider, traced to servers in Sydney and Melbourne data centres, halted train services and impeded emergency call handling, with the precise cause still unknown.

stories.title: Trains and emergency calls affected after major outage at Australia's largest telecoms company

clusters.headline: Young wine drinkers chill red wine in heatwave
clusters.summary: Young wine drinkers are abandoning the convention of serving red wine only at room temperature. The heatwave has prompted many to chill red wine. The long-held belief that red wine should be drunk at room temperature is being overturned by younger consumers seeking refreshment in hot weather.

stories.title: Why young wine drinkers are breaking the 'room temperature' red rule

clusters.headline: Judge Tabassum Khan threatened after convicting cow vigilantes
clusters.summary: Muslim judge Tabassum Khan faces online abuse and death threats in India after convicting 14 Hindu men in a lynching case involving 'cow vigilantes'. The convictions sparked the threats. The case relates to mob violence against those accused of cow slaughter, a sensitive issue in India.

stories.title: Muslim judge in India faces death threats after convicting 'cow vigilantes'

clusters.headline: Kenyan men challenge female surname ridicule
clusters.summary: In Kenya, men who inherit their father's first name as a surname are standing up to ridicule as naming traditions change. Children traditionally take the father's first name as a surname, but shifting customs are causing upset. Those with female-associated surnames are confronting mockery.

stories.title: How men with female surnames are standing up to ridicule in Kenya

clusters.headline: Next prime minister faces same fiscal issues
clusters.summary: The next prime minister will confront unchanged fiscal challenges despite a change in leadership. The person in charge of the country will change but the economic problems remain the same. Persistent budgetary constraints and stale growth outlooks define the inherited position.

stories.title: The economic challenges facing the next prime minister

clusters.headline: Starmer faces overseas war and party division
clusters.summary: In 2026, Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces war overseas while his party remains divided at home. British politicians traditionally unite during foreign crises, but this cohesion is absent. The Labour Party cannot find internal peace as international conflict intensifies.

stories.title: As Starmer faces war overseas, his party can't find peace at home
